events.task_name: S1 — NY Grad School Financial Aid Comparison (NYU / Columbia / Cornell Tech)
events.task_goal:
Your task: compare graduate school financial aid across three New York universities — NYU, Columbia University, and Cornell Tech — and summarize the findings.

For each school, find and record:
  - Types of aid available (fellowships, assistantships, scholarships, loans)
  - Typical funding amounts or stipends for PhD vs Master's students
  - Whether Master's students are commonly funded or self-funded
  - Any named fellowships or competitive awards
  - Application deadlines or requirements to be considered for aid

Steps:
1. Click the Google search box on screen, type 'NYU graduate financial aid', press Enter, then click the most relevant official NYU result and read it.
2. Go back to google.com, click the search box, type 'Columbia University graduate financial aid', press Enter, click the official Columbia result and read it.
3. Go back to google.com, click the search box, type 'Cornell Tech graduate financial aid', press Enter, click the official Cornell Tech result and read it.
4. Write a structured comparison with a section for each school, followed by a summary table comparing the three side by side.
Do NOT use any tools in your final response — just write the comparison text.

events.text: I'll click the Google search box to start searching for NYU graduate financial aid.
I'll click the Google search box and type the NYU query.
I'll type the NYU graduate financial aid search query.
I'll click the "Graduate Aid Application" link from New York University.
The NYU Graduate Aid Application page loaded. I'll click "Continue" to dismiss the cookie notice.
I'll click "Types of Aid" in the left sidebar to explore aid types.
I'll wait for the Types of Aid page to load fully.
I'll scroll down to read more about NYU's types of aid.
I can see NYU offers scholarships/grants, work-study, federal loans, and private loans. I'll click "Scholarships and Grants" to learn more.
I'll wait for the Scholarships and Grants page to load.
I'll click "Graduate Scholarships and Grants" to read about graduate-specific aid at NYU.
I can see the NYU Graduate Scholarships and Grants page. I'll scroll down to read more details.
I'll navigate to a more detailed NYU graduate funding page. Let me go back and look at the NYU graduate financial aid overview more broadly.
I'll wait for the Financial Aid and Scholarships main page to load.
I can see the NYU Financial Aid and Scholarships page. I'll click "Graduate Aid Application" in the sidebar.
The page is loading. I'll wait for it to fully load.
I can see the NYU Graduate Aid Application page. I'll scroll down to read more details about the types of aid and funding.
I can see important details about NYU graduate aid: FAFSA required, federal aid is primarily unsubsidized and Graduate PLUS loans, Federal Work-Study. I'll scroll down to read more.

Excellent! I can see important NYU information: Federal Loans, Federal Work-Study, Private Loans, and Graduate Assistantships (tuition remission + stipend for teaching/research). I'll scroll down to read more.
I can see Graduate Assistantships section clearly. Now I'll click on "Types of Aid" in the sidebar for more details.
I'll click "Types of Aid" to navigate to that page.
I can see the Types of Aid page showing scholarships/grants, student employment (federal work-study), federal loans, and private loans. I'll scroll down to read more about all the aid types.
I can see the Federal Work-Study information. Now I'll click on "Scholarships and Grants" in the sidebar to learn more about graduate scholarships.
I can see the page is loading for Scholarships and Grants. I'll wait and scroll up to see the content.
I can see the page shows primarily undergraduate scholarships and grants. I need to scroll down to find the graduate scholarships section.
I can see the NYU Promise is for undergraduates. I'll scroll down more to find the graduate section and Federal Grant Programs.
I can see Federal Grant Programs and State Scholarships. I'll continue scrolling to find graduate-specific scholarships and grants info.
I can see "Scholar Programs" at the bottom. I'll scroll down to read more about these programs, which are relevant to graduate students.

I can see Scholar Programs including Berkley Achievement Scholars, Breakthrough Scholars, Davis UWC Scholars, HEOP, and Lenape Scholarship. I'll scroll down to see if there are graduate-specific scholarships.

I can see more named scholarships: Lewis Rudin City Scholars, Martin Luther King Jr. Scholars Program, September 11 Scholarships. These appear to be primarily undergraduate programs. Now I'll navigate to a more specifically graduate-focused NYU financial aid page. Let me search for NYU Graduate School of Arts and Science financial aid for more specific PhD and Master's funding information.

I see the Federal Loans sidebar item was hovered over. Let me navigate to the NYU GSAS (Graduate School of Arts & Science) financial aid page for more specific graduate information. I'll open a new search.
I'm now on the NYU Federal Loans page. I can see there's a "Graduate" tab available. I'll click "Graduate" to see graduate-specific loan information.
I can see the "Graduate Federal Loan Options" section. I'll scroll down to read more details about graduate federal loan options.
I can see Federal Direct Unsubsidized Loans, Federal Direct PLUS Loan, Health Professions Student Loan Program, and Optional Loans. I'll click on "Federal Direct Unsubsidized Loans" to expand it.

Excellent! I can see important loan amounts: Federal Direct Unsubsidized Loans: $20,500 Graduates, $40,500 for Graduates in health professions, and effective July 1, 2026, $50,000 per year for professional degree students. I'll scroll down to see more.

I can see Federal Direct Unsubsidized Loans details (interest rate 7.94%, standard 10-year repayment). Now I have enough NYU information. Let me search for more specific NYU Graduate School PhD funding. I'll navigate to Google to search for NYU GSAS fellowship and stipend information.
I'll type the URL for Google to go back to search.
I'm on a new Google tab. I'll search for NYU GSAS PhD fellowship stipend information.
I'll type the NYU GSAS financial aid search query.

params.text: NYU GSAS graduate school PhD fellowship stipend financial aid

events.text: I can see very relevant results. I'll click "Graduate School Fellowships and Assistantships" from NYU GSAS.

I can see an AI Mode summary with very useful information about NYU GSAS. I can read key details: Health Insurance coverage, Fee Remission, Dissertation Support (Dean's Dissertation Fellowship). I can also see information about Teaching/Assistantships and exceptions (Data Science, Tisch Cinema Studies). Now let me click the actual NYU GSAS "Graduate School Fellowships and Assistantships" page link.
I can see at the bottom a link to "Graduate School Fellowships and Assistantships". I'll wait for the page to load.

I'm now on the NYU GSAS "Graduate School Fellowships and Assistantships" page. I can see sections for Doctoral Students (Henry M. MacCracken Program), Master's Students, Federal/State/Private Programs, and GSAS Tuition Incentive Program. I'll press Escape to dismiss the overlay, then scroll down to read more.
I can see the NYU GSAS Graduate School Fellowships and Assistantships page clearly now. I'll scroll down to read all details about the MacCracken Program and Master's student funding.

Excellent! I can now read the key details about the NYU MacCracken Program for doctoral students:
- 4 or 5 year award term
- Full tuition scholarship + registration/services fees
- Full health insurance coverage
- 9-month academic-year stipend for living expenses
- $1,000 Dean's Supplementary Fellowship Grant
- Students may also teach as adjunct instructors for additional pay

I'll scroll down to see more details about Master's students and funding amounts.

I can see the "Master's Students" section now. I can also see that some programs (Basic Medical Sciences, Cinema Studies, Data Science, Institute of Fine Arts, Study of the Ancient World) don't fund through MacCracken awards. I'll scroll down to read the Master's Students funding information.

Excellent! Now I can read key information about Master's Student funding at NYU GSAS:
- Financial aid is available in certain master's departments and programs
- Applicants must apply by the program's specified deadline
- No additional application forms needed for scholarship/fellowship consideration
- Master's students eligible for GSAS Tuition Incentive Program
- Programs available: GSAS Tuition Assistance for School Teachers, Resources for Military Veterans, Bachelor's-Master's Program, CAS/GSAS Tuition Program

I'll scroll down to read about the federal and private programs section.
I can see the Federal, State, and Private Programs section, as well as information about the GSAS Tuition Incentive Program. I'll scroll down to read about these programs.

Now I can read about the GSAS Tuition Incentive Program (TIP):
- GSAS provides tuition support to recipients of external awards
- Students receiving external awards of $13,000+ from recognized, academic sources can apply for matching tuition credits up to their outside award amount
- Students receiving external awards of less than $13,000 may apply for matching tuition credits up to 50% of the outside award amount

I'll scroll down to see more details about requirements and named fellowships.

I can now see important information about external fellowships that students can apply for through NYU's GSAS, including:
- The American-Scandinavian Foundation
- AmeriCorps
- The Amherst Memorial Fellowship
- The Beinecke Scholarship Program
- Canadian Institute of Health Research (CIHR)
- Colfuturo
- And many more

Key deadline info: Most external fellowship application deadlines are due in the fall, and funding begins the following fall. I'll scroll down to see more named fellowships.

I can see many external scholarships and fellowships students can apply for: Gates Millennium Scholarship, Beinecke Scholarship, DAAD, and many others. I now have enough information about NYU's graduate financial aid. Let me now navigate to Google to search for Columbia University's graduate financial aid.
I'm on a new Google tab. I'll click the search box and type the Columbia University graduate financial aid query.
I'll type the Columbia University graduate financial aid search query.
I can see multiple Columbia University results. I'll click "Financial Aid | GSAS" which is the most official and relevant Columbia result.
The Columbia GSAS Financial Aid page is loading. I'll wait for it to complete loading.
